name: Charlie Holmes
abstract: Charlie Holmes (January 27, 1910 near Boston, Massachusetts – September 19, 1985 in Stoughton, Massachusetts) was an American alto jazz saxophonist of the swing era. He also played clarinet and oboe for the Boston Civic Symphony in 1926.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Holmes was on third. And Tenny Hill when I joined the band in 29... Three Reeds, Greeley Walton, myself, and Charlie Holmes. And we had a solid match.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Luis Russell was the bandleader, and in the band was Paul Barbarin, Red Allen, Charlie Holmes, and Luis Russell playing piano

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Charlie Holmes as a contemporary player; Interviewee does not claim personal interaction, only acknowledges the name in the context of 'boys around there'.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: I'd know Carmen since the '30s, because she lived across the street from me, along with Charlie Holmes, Albert Nicholas. They all lived in the same apartment building.

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: I remember about the third or fourth day I was out of the Army, Charlie Holmes, you know, he came up to my house and he had a gig for me

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: though, it's everybody else in the band, but Louie; Biggy Madison, Charlie Holmes, Greeley, Louis Bacon, Ham Davis, Paul Barbara--
